<!-- Generated by a multi-agent design-research workflow (22 agents, ~3M tokens) on 2026-06-09.
     Source of truth for the game direction; see also the memory note game-design-direction. -->

# Making BrowserGame Good

## 1. North Star

**BrowserGame is a "drop-in action-RPG": a phone-friendly, browser-native, top-down ARPG where the satisfying kill â†’ loot â†’ equip â†’ harder-area loop happens in a shared, instanced world.** Lean Diablo (the loop and the feel), borrow RuneScape's *clarity and self-directed goals*, and use the WC3/SC2 lineage only for readable top-down combat staging. It is **not** a full MMO, not a sandbox skill-grind, and not a hero-arena.

I'm picking this because the codebase has *already voted* for it. The thing that's genuinely good today â€” the layered atmosphere stack, the server-authoritative combat, the portal-connected areas of escalating difficulty â€” is the skeleton of an ARPG, not the skeleton of a social MMO or a RuneScape clone. The ARPG loop "has no natural exit point" when every link is tight, and the work for a project like this is **tightening feedback and adding enemy variety at each link, not adding systems**. Meanwhile the real moat is zero-friction, phone-first, one-URL distribution â€” which is exactly this project's existing pillar and exactly what Diablo-likes have *never* delivered in a browser. That gap is the opportunity. The MMO/social layer (guilds, trade, global chat) is a *late* multiplier on a fun core, not the core itself; building it first would be furnishing a house with no floor.

The testable bar that keeps us honest: **a new player on a phone, with no tutorial, lands a satisfying kill within 15 seconds of the page loading.** That is measurable, it forces the phone-control and feedback work to the front, and â€” critically â€” it guards against polishing click-feel on a single enemy type. A satisfying kill needs something worth killing.

## 2. Honest current-state assessment

**Genuinely good (don't touch, don't second-guess):**
- **Netcode.** Textbook server-authoritative model: intent-only client, prediction + 100ms interpolation that converges (shared movement math), real AOI interest management, clean instancing with best-fit packing and GC. This is the hardest part of a multiplayer game and it's *done well*. (One real bug undermines it â€” see the moveScale item in NOW.)
- **Atmosphere & lighting.** The single most distinctive asset. Crossing into a fogged crypt at night with torchlight punching through darkness is the current "wow." Layered, theme-driven from the DB, performance-conscious.
- **Content-as-data.** Everything (areas, mobs, abilities, loot, themes) is SQLite, live-editable. New areas and mobs are *data*, not code. A force multiplier for everything in section 4.
- **Security posture & access tiers.** Scrypt accounts, capability-gated command registry, rate limits, payload caps. Done right, early.

**Missing for "fun" (the honest part):**
- **Every enemy fights identically.** This is the deepest hole and it's easy to under-rate. The 400-HP "boss" uses the same melee logic as a trash mob â€” it just has more HP. There is no ranged attacker, no telegraph, no behavioral variety. *No amount of hitstop saves a loop where every encounter is the same encounter.* Variety is what carries the loop past kill #20.
- **Progress evaporates.** State is RAM-only. A flaky phone disconnect or a server restart wipes the character. This silently invalidates *every* retention mechanic. The #1 structural hole.
- **The reward moments are silent and invisible.** Level-up, loot, gold â€” the entire ARPG dopamine engine â€” produce a text line at most. The renderer has the full FX vocabulary (floating numbers, crit numbers, bursts, shake, tint); it simply isn't wired to rewards. `sound.ts` `fromFx` handles *only* `cast` (`sound.ts:53`) â€” hits, deaths, and rewards are silent.
- **Combat lacks weight.** No hitstop, no impact SFX, no crits landing (the crit *render* path is fully built â€” see NOW), no telegraphs.
- **The economy is a faucet with no drain.** Gold flows in; nothing to spend it on. One progression track (level), gear gated by a single binary `mobLevel >= 5` flip. (Also: selling loot fires a *spell-cast* effect â€” `world.ts:256` â€” actively misleading feedback.)
- **Phone players can barely play.** The on-screen joystick is *computed but never drawn* (`input.ts` fully populates `joystick`; `main.ts` has only `drawMinimap`). Tapping the world only moves; you can attack only via a tiny HUD slot. On the primary platform, this is broken.
- **Onboarding is a cold open.** Fresh player spawns into an open world with a desktop-only hint and quests gated behind a typed `/accept` they can't discover.

The gap between "impressive engineering vertical slice" and "fun game" is almost entirely **enemy variety, feedback, persistence, and phone controls** â€” not new systems.

## 3. The core loop we commit to

**The 30-second loop (must feel incredible):**
> See a mob â†’ move/aim â†’ cast â†’ **hit lands with weight** (hitstop + flash + shake + sound, crits spike) â†’ mob dies (burst + sound) â†’ **loot pops with a sound and a sparkle** â†’ grab it â†’ it visibly made you stronger â†’ next mob.

Every arrow must produce proportional, satisfying feedback, and the *enemies* on the other end must differ enough that the feedback has something varied to land on. Today roughly half those arrows are silent and there is exactly one enemy archetype.

**The 30-minute loop (must give direction and a reason to return):**
> Walk up to a quest-giver NPC â†’ take an objective â†’ fight through to a harder area one portal away â†’ kill a boss with a *telegraphed, learnable* mechanic â†’ get a rarer drop (structured randomness, soft pity floor) â†’ return, turn in, **spend gold at the vendor on a guaranteed baseline upgrade** â†’ level ticks up â†’ a new area/quest is now reachable â†’ and **all of this is still here when you come back tomorrow.**

Two interleaved progression tracks (character level + gear), with quest/area completion as connective tissue â€” the "progression sandwich" so a stall in one track is never a dead wall.

## 4. Prioritized roadmap

Effort: **S** â‰ˆ hoursâ€“1 day, **M** â‰ˆ 2â€“4 days, **L** â‰ˆ ~1 week. Impact as labeled.

---

### NOW â€” next 1â€“2 weeks

These are the critic's top picks. Most are verified-cheap because the hard part already exists.

**Theme: Combat & Game Feel**

- **Wire reward + combat sound and FX off existing channels.** *What:* loot/gold/level-up/hit/death stings and bursts. *Why:* the single highest impact-per-hour win â€” the dopamine engine is built and switched off. *How:* branch `sound.ts` `fromFx` beyond the `cast`-only path at `sound.ts:53` onto `hit`/`death`/`melee`; fire reward stings off the `drainNotices` level-up/quest channel; push new FxEvent kinds in `onMobKilled`/`tickItems`/`progressQuests` and render via the existing floating-number/burst path. Lazy-load tiny CC0 clips to protect the payload budget. **Fold in the sell-FX correctness fix here** (next item) â€” same code touch. *Impact: high Â· Effort: S.*
- **Fix the misleading sell feedback + dedup the sell path.** *What:* selling loot currently fires a *spell-cast* FX (`world.ts:256`); give it its own coin/gold FxEvent instead, and route `interact()`'s sell through the tested `vendor.sellAll()` rather than its re-implemented loop. *Why:* it's a correctness bug (wrong feedback) and an anti-bloat duplication, both fixed in the same touch as the reward-FX work. Leave the `EQUIPMENT`/`SELL_VALUES` seed constants intact; delete the unused `rollEquipDrop()`. *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*
- **Add the server-side crit roll.** *What:* roll a crit chance, multiply damage, set `ev.crit = true`. *Why:* a reward spike on every swing â€” and **the entire render path is already built** (`combat.ts:156-157` carries the flag; `pixi-renderer.ts:633-647` already draws crits bigger, in hot orange-red, with extra rise). Only the server roll is missing. *How:* a few lines in the damage path; it drives the existing heavy-juice tier for free. *Impact: high Â· Effort: S.*
- **Per-hit hitstop + proportional shake + victim squash, driven by existing FxEvents.** *What:* on a `kind:'hit'` event, briefly freeze *only* the two involved sprites, kick shake scaled to damage, pop a 1â€“3 frame scale-up on the victim. *How:* extend `pixi-renderer.ts` `updateFx`; the wire already carries hit events with value and crit flag. **Never pause the sim or peers** â€” client presentation only. Cap total shake; minimize on DoT ticks. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*

**Theme: Enemy Variety (pulled forward â€” the loop depends on it)**

- **One ranged mob + one telegraphed boss attack.** *What:* a single ranged enemy archetype and a single windup-then-strike boss attack with a floor decal. *Why:* this is the most fun-relevant change in the entire report â€” the 30-minute loop explicitly hinges on "kill the boss with a telegraphed, learnable mechanic," and right now every enemy is identical. Juice lands far better on two distinct enemies than on one. *How:* the ranged mob **reuses the existing projectile system end-to-end** (`world.ts:482-489` spawns projectiles; `schema.ts` already supports `kind:'projectile'` abilities) â€” it's data plus a behavior branch, not new tech. The boss telegraph emits a windup FxEvent and applies damage only when `windupMs` (a DB column) elapses. Scope to exactly these two; broader behavior work is NEXT. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*

**Theme: Progression & Persistence**

- **Persist character progression to SQLite â€” including guests via an opaque token.** *What:* save `PlayerSave` (level/xp/gold/loot/gear/quests) on disconnect + periodic flush; reload on login. *Why:* the largest structural retention hole. **Critical reconciliation:** since the deferred-account model (NEXT) makes the *default* player a nameless guest, persisting only logged-in accounts would retain almost nobody. So guests get a **server-issued opaque token stored client-side (localStorage)**; a returning phone keeps its character without an account, and "create account" later *claims* that token's character. *How:* `exportPlayer`/`importPlayer` (`world.ts:398/419`) already serialize the exact shape and `importPlayer` clamps untrusted input; add a `characters` table keyed by account-or-token, write from the `index.ts` close handler + an autosave tick. **Sequencing:** ship persistence *before/with* the heartbeat below, so a phone hiccup can't tear down an unsaved character. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*
- **Vendor SELLS gear (the gold sink).** *What:* let the Merchant sell a small fixed stock of floor-tier gear + a heal consumable. *Why:* gold is a faucet with no drain; *guaranteed baseline* gear is the safe deterministic complement to random drops â€” never best-in-slot. *How:* add `{t:'buy';itemId}` to `protocol.ts`, `World.buy()` beside `interact()` (re-validate gold + stock server-side), reuse the existing bag UI. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*

**Theme: Onboarding, UX & Phone**

- **Draw the on-screen joystick.** *What:* render the base+knob `input.ts` already computes. *Why:* phone-first is the moat; an invisible control fails the 15-second bar. *How:* `drawJoystick()` in `main.ts` reading `input.joystick`, same pattern as `drawMinimap` (`main.ts:442`). *Impact: high Â· Effort: S.*
- **Tap-to-attack on the game canvas.** *What:* a quick tap (vs. a drag) casts the selected ability at the tapped point / nearest mob. *Why:* phone players currently can't attack while moving; auto-target assist already exists in `computeAim()`. *How:* disambiguate tap from joystick-drag by distance+time threshold in the `main.ts` touch handler. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*
- **Quest-givers via the existing `npc.kind` seam + co-locate the town plaza.** *What:* a `questgiver` branch in `World.interact()` so walk-up + E offers/turns in quests (kill the typed `/accept`); cluster vendor + quest-giver + portal + respawn near spawn. *Why:* `/accept` is undiscoverable; onboarding needs a discoverable in-world next action; a "persistent recurring encounter" (FFXIV Limsa) is the cheapest social-retention lever. *How:* reuse `acceptQuest`/`progressQuests`, add a quest-giver NPC in `seed.ts`, one-line offer/turn-in (no dialogue trees); co-location is pure coordinate edits in `seed.ts`/`areas.ts`. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M + S.*
- **Input-aware hotbar + hints + a "first objective" beacon.** *What:* on coarse pointers, drop key-letter glyphs, swap hint text to "Drag to move Â· Tap to attack," and draw a pulsing ring over the nearest mob with a one-liner. *How:* `matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)')` + a state-driven hint picker in `drawHud`. *Impact: high Â· Effort: Sâ€“M.*

**Theme: Tech Foundation**

- **Fix the weather moveScale prediction divergence.** *What:* thread the authoritative `moveScale` onto the `you` packet and multiply it into `predictor.ts` `apply()`. *Why:* **verified real** â€” `world.ts` applies `PLAYER_SPEED * moveScale` (the field lives at `world.ts:165`, applied at `:186`), but `predictor.ts:73` ignores it, so under any non-1.0 weather the local player rubber-bands on every reconcile. *How:* read it in `apply()`; add a predictor test asserting parity with `World` under `moveScale â‰  1`. *Impact: high Â· Effort: S.*
- **WebSocket heartbeat with a *generous* timeout + reconnect grace + send-backpressure guard.** *What:* ping/pong to reap genuinely-dead sockets; skip a client's snapshot if its `bufferedAmount` is backed up (snapshots are absolute, so dropping one is harmless). *Why:* flaky mobile networks leak instances and self-DoS slow clients â€” **but an aggressive reaper is exactly what turns a recoverable phone hiccup into a disconnect.** So the timeout is tens of seconds, with a short reconnect-grace window where the character isn't immediately torn down. This is why persistence ships first. *How:* ws ping interval + a `bufferedAmount` check in the `index.ts` broadcast loop. *Impact: high Â· Effort: S.*

---

### NEXT â€” the depth pass (after the core feels good and persists)

**Combat & Game Feel**
- **Differentiated mob behavior + a real boss (full pass).** Generalize the NOW slice into a `behavior` enum on `MobTemplate` (`melee`/`ranged`/`boss`) branched in the pure `stepMob`; the boss requests a telegraphed AoE. Scope to 2â€“3 behaviors, data-driven. (The single ranged mob + single telegraph already shipped in NOW; this widens it.) *Impact: high Â· Effort: Mâ€“L.*
- **Client-side ability input buffer** â€” queue a press made during cooldown, fire it the instant it's legal; server still re-validates. Pure perceived tightness. *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*
- **Player-took-damage shake + red vignette pulse** derived from the hp delta the client already sees. *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*

**Progression & Itemization**
- **Item rarity tiers + soft bad-luck pity floor.** A rarity enum that scales existing power/hp stats, colors the drop glint, and a per-player dry-streak counter (persisted) that guarantees an uncommon+ after a streak. Structured randomness is the retention sweet spot. *Avoid a full affix engine.* *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*
- **A short gear ladder tied to area/boss farmed.** Expand 2 â†’ 3â€“4 tiers as data rows; higher tiers on crypt mobs, a boss-only guaranteed drop. Agency over what you chase; no engine change. *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*

**Content & Social**
- **Quest objective types beyond kill-N** (collect-N, talk-to-NPC) via `QuestDef`. Re-validate collected quantities server-side at turn-in. *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*
- **/whisper (only).** Add an optional `to` to the chat message; implement `/whisper` in the command registry, rate-limited via the existing bucket. *Party chat is deliberately deferred* â€” there is no grouping system, so a `/p` channel would either smuggle in a whole grouping feature or be a channel with no members. Build grouping first, separately, if it ever earns its place. *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*
- **Zero-friction "Play now" + deferred account creation.** Name overlay â†’ spawn immediately (works with the guest-token persistence above); offer account creation on first level-up (dismissible, *claims* the existing guest character). *Impact: high Â· Effort: M.*

**Tech Foundation**
- **Decouple input send-rate** â€” send on input-change edges + a low-rate keepalive instead of fixed 30Hz; keep prediction at 30Hz. Pure upstream savings on phones, no per-client server state. *This is the phone-bandwidth lever; it's why delta-encoding is not here.* *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*
- **Reuse one SpatialGrid per tick** (add `clear()`), eliminating per-instance per-frame allocation. Cover with a test so instances don't leak entities into each other's AOI. *Impact: low Â· Effort: S.*
- **Measure the bundle + add a CI size check at current size + headroom.** *What:* run `vite build`, record the real production size, add a one-line CI assertion at that size plus headroom. *Why:* the prior plan asserted an "8MB budget" without ever measuring, and proposed a lazy-asset/procedural-fallback pipeline before any sprite atlas exists. Measure first; the renderer is still procedural shapes. The full lazy-asset pipeline is deferred until sprite atlases are actually added (it's coupled to the unsettled sprite-art decision anyway). *Impact: medium Â· Effort: S.*

---

### LATER â€” multipliers on a proven core (do not start early)

- **Server-authoritative atomic trade** between nearby players â€” both-sides-locked, single transaction, re-validated at execution. The biggest exploit surface; **never two one-way gives** (money-doubling). *Effort: L.*
- **Lightweight guild/clan** â€” name + roster + guild chat, single membership, **no power buffs** (WoW Cataclysm member-count abuse). Depends on persistence. *Effort: L.*
- **Rotating world boss / seasonal event** as timed SQLite rows funneling players into a shared area, reusing day/night + theme. Server-scheduled only; account-wide value, not a solo grind. *Effort: M.*
- **End-of-fight commendation â†’ cosmetic title.** Positive-reinforcement social, harassment-free. **No DPS meters/inspect/leaderboards.** *Effort: M.*
- **Delta-encode snapshots** â€” **gated behind a measured bandwidth ceiling actually being hit with real concurrent players.** Stateful per-client name/hue caching plus AOI entry/exit and area-change reset is a documented bug magnet (stale fields after AOI churn); for a handful of players it trades two pillars (simple-over-clever, one-source-of-truth) for a problem we don't yet have. The cheaper `decouple input send-rate` (NEXT) already covers phone upstream. Do not add per-client snapshot state until a profiler demands it. *Effort: M.*
- **Smoothed, slightly aim-leading camera** â€” lerp instead of hard-snap, modest look-ahead. Verify on a phone viewport. *Effort: S.*

*(Cut from the roadmap entirely: per-instance server tick-timestamp time-sync. It contradicts our own current-state assessment that 100ms interpolation already converges, and section 6 forbids NTP-style sync. Revisit only if variable-RTT rubber-banding is actually observed on a phone.)*

## 5. The first 5 things to build

A solo dev can ship all five this week, and the game will *feel* dramatically better:

1. **Wire reward + combat sound and FX** (and fold in the sell-fires-a-cast fix + sell-path dedup). (S) The silent dopamine engine turns on, and a misleading-feedback bug dies in the same touch.
2. **Add the server-side crit roll.** (S) The render path is already built (`pixi-renderer.ts:633-647`); a few lines give every swing a reward spike.
3. **One ranged mob + one telegraphed boss attack** via the existing projectile path. (M) The loop finally has variety to make the juice land.
4. **Draw the joystick + tap-to-attack** in `main.ts`. (Sâ€“M) The primary platform becomes actually playable.
5. **Persist characters to SQLite** (with the guest opaque-token so phone guests keep progress) and **fix the weather moveScale prediction bug**. (M + S) Progress stops evaporating; the one persistent rubber-band on our best system goes away.

(Honorable mention, both S and pure data: co-locate the town plaza and add the vendor gold sink.)

## 6. What NOT to do

- **Don't polish click-feel before adding enemy variety.** Tuning hitstop/shake/flash on a combat system with exactly one enemy behavior is polishing a loop nobody will play to kill #20. One ranged mob and one telegraph come *with* the juice, not after it.
- **Don't build the MMO social layer first.** Guilds, trade, global chat, friends lists are *multipliers on a fun core*, not the core. Social-before-fun is furnishing a house with no floor.
- **Don't ship persistence without solving guest identity.** Persisting only accounts while the default player is a nameless guest retains almost nobody â€” the two features silently cancel. Guests persist via a client-stored opaque token.
- **Don't pair an aggressive socket reaper with persistence.** A short timeout turns a recoverable phone hiccup into a disconnect-and-wipe. Generous timeout + reconnect grace; persistence ships first.
- **Don't make best-in-slot buyable or guaranteed.** A deterministic BiS shop / auction house kills the dopamine prediction-error â€” the literal D3 auction-house failure. Vendor sells *floor-tier baseline* only; rares stay drop-only.
- **Don't pause the sim for hitstop.** In a shared world that stutters every nearby player. Hitstop freezes *only the two involved sprites, client-side* â€” the server keeps ticking.
- **Don't add a single global chat channel, and don't ship party chat without parties.** Undifferentiated global is noise; a `/p` channel with no grouping system is either incoherent or a hidden grouping feature smuggled in under "chat." Ship `/whisper` alone.
- **Don't ship a single progression track.** Any stall in a lone track = quit. Level + gear (+ quest/area) *interleaved* before more content.
- **Don't over-juice.** Long hitstop or heavy shake on every weak tick (DoT, auto-attacks) is nauseating. Scale with hit weight; cap the total. One shake/flash pipeline â€” don't build a second juice system alongside `atmosphere.ts`.
- **Don't over-engineer the depth features.** No affix DSL (a rarity enum scaling existing stats is enough), no mob scripting language (2â€“3 behavior enums), no NTP-style clock sync, no stateful delta-encoding until a profiler demands it, no full binary protocol yet.
- **Don't build a payload-budget enforcement pipeline before measuring the bundle.** Measure the real `vite build` size, gate CI at that size plus headroom, and defer lazy-asset swapping until sprite atlases actually exist.
- **Don't add coercive daily-login/streak FOMO.** Obligation logins clash with intrinsic motivation and with this project's "simple, respectful, phone-friendly drop-in" pillars.
- **Don't add DPS meters, gear-inspect, or public leaderboards.** Harassment vectors needing moderation a small team won't have.
- **Don't break the one-URL phone pillar.** It's the actual moat. Guard the (measured) payload budget, keep guests login-free, never make precise manual aim the only control scheme. Anything that breaks `npm run dev` from a phone is a bug, not a tradeoff.
- **Don't build trade as two one-way "gives," ever.** When you eventually build it, one atomic, both-confirmed, server-re-validated swap. The project's biggest future exploit surface.
- **Don't start the LATER tier early.** World bosses, seasonal resets, guilds, atomic trade, and delta-encoding all presume a deep, persisted, populated base. Building them now is un-wired future-proofing against the anti-bloat doctrine.
